narration: Right now, 42% of Americans have an unread financial statement sitting in their inbox. That's not laziness — it's a documented psychological pattern. And it has a price tag you're not going to like.
visual: Dark background. Large envelope SVG with NEW badge animating in. Counter ticking up from 0% to 42% in bold red. Three notification badges slide in stacked below — 'Credit Card Statement', 'Bank Alert: Low Balance', 'Overdue Payment Notice'.

narration: First hit: late fees. Avoiders miss two or three payments a year — that's $40 a pop and a credit score hit that follows you for seven years. And this is just the entry cost of the habit.
visual: Light background. Monthly calendar grid with red X marks appearing on three dates. Pink piggy bank SVG with a crack. '$40 LATE FEE x 3' badge pops in. Running dollar total animates up to $120.

narration: Then there's missed fraud. Banks only cover unauthorized charges if you report them within 60 days. Avoiders blow right past that window — and the average missed charge is $247, completely gone.
visual: Dark background. Credit card SVG slides in with red X over it. 60-day countdown bar drains to empty. '$247 GONE FOREVER' badge springs in. Warning message flashes twice.

narration: Here's a wild one — 80% of medical bills contain errors. That's not a typo. If you're an avoider, you're literally paying for things you never received, and you'll never even know it.
visual: Light background. Medical bill document slides up with line items. Three error items highlighted with red alert dots appearing in sequence — 'Lab Work x3 billed', 'Duplicate Procedure', 'Insurance Error'. '80%' counter animates in large.

narration: And the IRS has zero sympathy for avoidance anxiety. Miss a filing deadline and you're paying 5% per month in penalties — up to 25% of your total bill. That turns $2,000 into $2,500 in just six months.
visual: Dark background. IRS-style letter with eagle emblem. Red penalty meter bar grows from 0% to 25%. Dollar amount animates from $2,000 crossed out to $2,500 in red.

narration: Add it all up over ten years — late fees, missed fraud, billing errors, IRS penalties — and financial avoidance costs you $24,000. The fix? Ten minutes, once a month. Open it. Review it. Protect yourself.
visual: Light background. Cost breakdown list itemizes each cost fading in one by one. Large red '$24,000' counter animates up. Red clock SVG. Bold CTA: '10 MINUTES ONCE A MONTH' with 'OPEN · REVIEW · PROTECT'.
